description: A skilled developer specializing in smart contracts, decentralized applications, and automated blockchain execution.

questions.question: What is a smart contract?
questions.answer: Self-executing code on the blockchain

questions.question: Which programming language is commonly used for Ethereum smart contracts?
questions.answer: Solidity

questions.question: What does 'gas' refer to in smart contract execution?
options: The computational fee for executing operations
The speed of the blockchain network
questions.answer: The computational fee for executing operations

questions.question: What happens when a smart contract is deployed to the blockchain?
options: It becomes immutable and permanently stored
It can be easily modified by the owner
It automatically gets deleted after use
questions.answer: It becomes immutable and permanently stored

questions.question: What is the main advantage of smart contracts over traditional contracts?
options: They can be written in any language
questions.answer: Automatic execution without intermediaries

questions.question: What is an ERC-20 token?
options: A standard for fungible tokens on Ethereum
questions.answer: A standard for fungible tokens on Ethereum

questions.question: What is a constructor in Solidity?
options: A function that runs once when the contract is deployed
A function that destroys the contract
A function that updates contract code
questions.answer: A function that runs once when the contract is deployed

questions.question: What is a modifier in Solidity?
options: Code that can be reused to change function behavior
questions.answer: Code that can be reused to change function behavior

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'require' statement in Solidity?
options: To validate conditions and revert if false
questions.answer: To validate conditions and revert if false

questions.question: What is a fallback function in Solidity?
options: A function called when no other function matches
A function that backs up data
questions.answer: A function called when no other function matches

questions.question: What is reentrancy in smart contracts?
options: A vulnerability where a function calls itself recursively
A method to optimize gas usage
questions.answer: A vulnerability where a function calls itself recursively

questions.question: What is the difference between 'view' and 'pure' functions?
options: View reads state, pure doesn't read or modify state
View modifies state, pure reads state
questions.answer: View reads state, pure doesn't read or modify state

questions.question: What is a struct in Solidity?
options: A custom data type that groups variables
questions.answer: A custom data type that groups variables

questions.question: What is an event in Solidity?
options: A way to log data that external applications can listen to
questions.answer: A way to log data that external applications can listen to

questions.question: What is the 'payable' keyword used for?
options: To allow a function to receive Ether
To make a function cost gas
To mark a function as expensive
questions.answer: To allow a function to receive Ether

questions.question: What is a mapping in Solidity?
options: A function that converts data types
questions.answer: A key-value data structure

questions.question: What is the difference between 'memory' and 'storage' in Solidity?
options: Memory is temporary, storage is permanent
Memory is permanent, storage is temporary
questions.answer: Memory is temporary, storage is permanent

questions.question: What is an interface in Solidity?
options: A contract that defines function signatures without implementation
A user interface for smart contracts
questions.answer: A contract that defines function signatures without implementation

questions.question: What is inheritance in Solidity?
options: A way for contracts to derive properties from other contracts
questions.answer: A way for contracts to derive properties from other contracts

questions.question: What is a library in Solidity?
options: Reusable code that can be deployed once and used by multiple contracts
questions.answer: Reusable code that can be deployed once and used by multiple contracts

questions.question: What is the 'selfdestruct' function?
options: A function that destroys a contract and sends remaining Ether
A function that backs up contract data
A function that pauses contract execution
questions.answer: A function that destroys a contract and sends remaining Ether

questions.question: What is a proxy pattern in smart contracts?
options: A way to upgrade smart contracts while keeping the same address
A security feature to hide contract code
A method to reduce gas costs
questions.answer: A way to upgrade smart contracts while keeping the same address

questions.question: What is the diamond pattern?
options: A modular smart contract architecture pattern
questions.answer: A modular smart contract architecture pattern

questions.question: What is a factory pattern in smart contracts?
options: A contract that creates other contracts
questions.answer: A contract that creates other contracts

questions.question: What is OpenZeppelin?
options: A library of secure smart contract templates
questions.answer: A library of secure smart contract templates

questions.question: What is Truffle?
questions.answer: A development framework for Ethereum

questions.question: What is Hardhat?
questions.answer: An Ethereum development environment

questions.question: What is Web3.js?
options: A JavaScript library for interacting with Ethereum
questions.answer: A JavaScript library for interacting with Ethereum

questions.question: What is an oracle in blockchain?
options: A service that provides external data to smart contracts
questions.answer: A service that provides external data to smart contracts

questions.question: What is a decentralized exchange (DEX)?
options: A trading platform built on smart contracts
questions.answer: A trading platform built on smart contracts

questions.question: What is yield farming?
options: Earning rewards by providing liquidity to DeFi protocols
questions.answer: Earning rewards by providing liquidity to DeFi protocols

questions.question: What is a liquidity pool?
options: A smart contract holding tokens for decentralized trading
questions.answer: A smart contract holding tokens for decentralized trading
